description: A designed 'Worst of the Week' rogues-gallery collage card in portrait format on a black textured background. Twelve booking-style mugshot photographs of men are arranged in a grid (four columns, three rows, with the center positions occupied by the title block). Each mugshot has a small dark caption bar beneath it naming an alleged crime: top row 'Child Sex Abuse', 'Manslaughter', 'Robbery', 'Stalking'; middle 'Drug Possession', then the logo, then 'Child Sex Abuse'; next rows 'Aggravated Assault' and 'Manslaughter'; bottom row 'Homicide', 'Child Sex Abuse', 'Manslaughter', 'Attempted Murder'. The men appear to be of varied ages and ethnicities, photographed against jail/booking backgrounds (one shows a height-measurement scale). The centerpiece is large white serif/script branding 'Worst of the Week' with 'of the' set on a red banner. A bottom-left red bar contains the URL 'ice.gov/WoW' in white, and the bottom-right shows the round DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark. The propaganda technique is a fear-and-threat montage: grouping crime labels with faces to suggest immigrants are dangerous criminals and to encourage the public to 'SHARE their information' (per the tweet). Target audience: the general public, to build support for aggressive immigration enforcement by emphasizing severe violent and sexual offenses.
notable_text: Worst of the Week; ice.gov/WoW; crime labels: Child Sex Abuse, Manslaughter, Robbery, Stalking, Drug Possession, Aggravated Assault, Homicide, Attempted Murder
